---
title: Pruning fruit trees
date: 2024-01-28
tags: [gardening, orchard]
---

Late winter is the season, when the trees are still dormant but the worst frost is past. I prune apples and pears for an open, vase-like shape with plenty of light in the center. Stone fruit like peaches and plums gets the same treatment but with less aggressive cuts.

The three-cut method on larger branches prevents bark tearing. A shallow undercut eighteen inches out, a top cut a little farther from the trunk, and a final flush cut to the branch collar removes the limb cleanly. A torn strip of bark running down the trunk is an invitation to disease.

Water sprouts and crossing branches come out first, then any limb growing inward or downward. I try to remember that the goal is not the prettiest tree but the healthiest fruit, and that stepping back and squinting every few cuts keeps me from overdoing it.
